items.title: Menopause and perimenopause support expands into nationwide clinics

children.content: Access to physiotherapy, dietetics and psychology services will become routine in a national expansion to menopause and perimenopause support from July 1.

articles.title: Pre-made plans falter as England labours again

children.content: England came into the World Cup with a side it thought was capable of beating the best teams in the world. If it can't beat the little guys, it may never get a chance to prove its theory right.

articles.title: History beckons but a 'fearless' opponent awaits the Socceroos

children.content: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.

image.altText: Forward Mohamed Salah of Egypt talks with midfielder Mohammad Mohebbi of IR Iran

children.content: The Three Lions have already booked a place in the World Cup's knockout stages, but they will be looking to tie up top spot in Group L against Panama, as Ghana and Croatia lock horns in Philadelphia. Follow live.

items.title: Brisbane man makes fastest human-powered Everest ascent from sea level

children.content: Avalanches, altitude sickness, orcs and Justin Bieber — a Brisbane mountaineer is believed to have set a new world record on Mount Everest for charity, despite every obstacle thrown his way.

items.title: Shark-spotting drones to monitor 70 NSW beaches every day of the year

children.content: Shark-spotting drones will provide year-round coverage at about 70 beaches along the NSW coast as part of an expanded surveillance program announced after a Sydney mother was attacked by a suspected white shark.

items.title: Murder trial mementos: The Chamberlain artefacts too sensitive to exhibit

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

items.title: How many products in your daily life are made from fossil fuels?

items.title: 'Very thankful': Mum of child with amputation grateful for community support

items.title: Like many war heroes, the man who won this medal never talked about it

children.content: Private Michael Paice never talked about his distinguished medal from World War II, but his story of extraordinary bravery is now being told.

items.title: As the cost of living rises, old-school bartering makes a comeback

children.content: Bartering is one of the oldest trade systems in the world, and as the cost of living soars, there is growing interest in trading homegrown and homemade goods.

items.title: Wallabies and All Blacks locked in for 123-year Bledisloe Cup first

children.content: For the first time in 123 years, the Wallabies and All Blacks will play a Bledisloe Cup Test on Anzac Day, with Lang Park hosting in 2027, 2029 and 2031.

items.title: Paralysed man walks again after golf 'lit a fire' in recovery from bike crash

children.content: A Sunshine Coast man who was told he would never walk again after a bicycle accident now wants golf to be included in the 2032 Paralympic Games, crediting the sport with his recovery.

items.title: Autistic talent tapped to help fill growing cybersecurity skills gap

children.content: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.
